hero.name: Atlas
hero.tagline: An honest map of the AI tools operators actually ship with
hero.description: Hand-tested AI tools, rated by the StoicSoft team after at least four weeks in real product work. No benchmark-farming, no affiliate bumps.

items.name: Aether
items.description: Our own AI workspace — cites every answer with the doc lines it pulled from. The tool our operators run Monday planning on.

items.name: StoicSoft Copilot
items.description: A thin layer over Aether that specialises in product copy — changelogs, release notes, pricing page drafts. Used across every StoicSoft surface.

items.name: Helix Reconciler
items.description: Reads bank statements, matches them to your ledger, flags the exceptions. Built inside Helix, our non-custodial payments rail.

items.name: 1DevTool Pair
items.tagline: Pairs with you while you scaffold
items.description: An opinionated coding assistant tuned to our template registry. Shows you the starter that fits, instead of generating a whole new one.

items.name: ServerCompass Ranger
items.description: Summarises alerts, correlates probes, drafts the first runbook comment. Slack-native, built on top of the ServerCompass probe log.

items.name: Faro Crop
items.description: A small tool that respects the rule of thirds better than the generic cropper. Built for the photojournal template.

items.name: Margin Transcribe
items.description: Built for Signal FM episode transcripts. Handles two-speaker conversations, punctuation, and speaker labelling better than the defaults.

items.name: Quill Write Mode
items.description: Inside the Quill markdown editor. Sits quietly; only speaks when you press a key combo. Built for long-form essays.

items.name: Tide Classifier
items.description: Classifies CSVs, tickets, and Slack threads into categories you define. Built for small ops teams who can’t afford a data team.

items.name: Halo Reflect
items.tagline: Journaling prompts that don’t feel like a questionnaire
items.description: A weekly prompt engine inside the Halo habit app. The only AI feature we shipped that people write us to thank us for.

items.name: Open Roots Vision
items.description: A nonprofit-facing computer vision model that looks at field photos and estimates sapling survival rates. Open source, MIT.

items.name: Privacy Ping
items.tagline: Scans your prompts before they leave
items.description: An on-device guardrail that masks PII before AI calls go to third-party providers. Built for StoicSoft internal use, open-sourced as MIT.
